If you haven’t already, check out our [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/Expensify/ReactNativeChat/blob/main/contributingGuides/CONTRIBUTING.md) for onboarding and email contributors@expensify.com to request to join our Slack channel! ___ **Version Number:** v9.3.37-9 **Reproducible in staging?:** Yes **Reproducible in production?:** Yes **If this was caught during regression testing, add the test name, ID and link from BrowserStack:** KI retest https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues/78292 **Email or phone of affected tester (no customers):** new public account **Issue reported by:** Applause Internal Team **Bug source:** Regression TC Execution **Device used:** MacOS Monterey 12.7.4 / Chrome **App Component:** Other ## Action Performed: Precondition: on the web browser logged in with OD >> create a workspace and add expensifail account B as a member Steps to reproduce: 1. Open the link www.staging.new.expensify.com 2. Create a new account with a public domain email address 3.In the modal “What’s your work email?”, enter the email address of account B (the same account invited in OD) 4. Click "Add work email" 5, Enter magic code ## Expected Result: Email added. User can proceed with onboarding. ## Actual Result: Modal shown: Couldn’t add work email We couldn’t add applausetester+bp1503mem2@applause.expensifail.com. Please try again later in Settings or chat with Concierge for guidance. ## Workaround: Unknown ## Platforms: - [ ] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [ ] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [x] Windows: Chrome - [x] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/90a7575e-547f-4d45-8b1e-93bda5eac2bc [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)

body:
If you haven’t already, check out our [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/Expensify/ReactNativeChat/blob/main/contributingGuides/CONTRIBUTING.md) for onboarding and email contributors@expensify.com to request to join our Slack channel! ___ **Version Number:** 9.3.36-10 **Reproducible in staging?:** Needs Reproduction (Unable to reproduce) **Reproducible in production?:** Needs Reproduction **If this was caught during regression testing, add the test name, ID and link from BrowserStack:** **Email or phone of affected tester (no customers):** **Logs:** https://stackoverflow.com/c/expensify/questions/4856 **Expensify/Expensify Issue URL:** **Issue reported by:** @timothysmall **Slack conversation** (hyperlinked to channel name): [#Expensify Bugs](https://expensify.slack.com/archives/C049HHMV9SM/p1773412325168159) ## Action Performed: 1. Payer opens a report needing reimbursement 2. Payer clicks Pay on the report (expecting to reimburse the employee) 3. Observe the report status change ## Expected Result: Clicking Pay reimburses the employee (sends actual payment), matching Classic behavior. ## Actual Result: The report is "marked as Paid" ## Workaround: Unknown ## Platforms: Select the officially supported platforms where the issue was reproduced: - [ ] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [ ] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [x] Windows: Chrome - [x] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Notes: Here is what Melvin said about this issue: Recommendation This should be filed as a bug in Expensify/App to improve the onlyShowPayElsewhere fallback behavior — specifically to avoid silently offering “Pay elsewhere” when the policy is configured for ACH reimbursement and the required data simply hasn’t loaded yet. For this specific user, the issue was transient and is unlikely to be consistently reproducible. ## Screenshots/Videos The screenshot for the issue is in Slack [thread](https://expensify.slack.com/archives/C049HHMV9SM/p1773412325168159) [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)

body:
If you haven’t already, check out our [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/Expensify/ReactNativeChat/blob/main/contributingGuides/CONTRIBUTING.md) for onboarding and email contributors@expensify.com to request to join our Slack channel! ___ **Version Number:** 9.3.36-10 **Reproducible in staging?:** Needs Reproduction (Unable to reproduce) **Reproducible in production?:** Needs Reproduction **If this was caught during regression testing, add the test name, ID and link from BrowserStack:** **Email or phone of affected tester (no customers):** **Logs:** https://stackoverflow.com/c/expensify/questions/4856 **Expensify/Expensify Issue URL:** **Issue reported by:** @marianaabussamra **Slack conversation** (hyperlinked to channel name): [#Expensify Bugs](https://expensify.slack.com/archives/C049HHMV9SM/p1773503748822459) ## Action Performed: 1. The admin goes to Workspace 2. Clicks on the Workspace Name 3. Goes to the Expensify Card tab 4. Clicks Issue card 5. Select the user they're trying to assign a card to 6. Picks the physical card option 7. Enter the Smart Limit amount and click Next 8. This triggers a Magic code that is sent to their email 9. They enter the email received, and this is where they get the error message ## Expected Result: They are adding the correct number, and the error message doesn't say it's incorrect, so they should be able to issue the card ## Actual Result: After entering the 6-digit magic code, they get a generic error message and can't issue the card ## Workaround: Unknown ## Platforms: Select the officially supported platforms where the issue was reproduced: - [ ] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [ ] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [x] Windows: Chrome - [x] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)
